messages.subject: [R-sig-DB] R GUI is puzzling me
messages.body_plain:
Dear Sir/Madam,

I have a data.frame which consist of two columns and several rows. The
values of 2nd column can be either TRUE or FALSE.
I want to save all the value of first column in another variable whose
corresponding row in second column have value TRUE.

Please consider the following example:

#currently i am doing this.
date <- 0
j <- 1
for (i in 1:nrow(final))
{
    if(final[i,2] == TRUE)
    {
        date[j] <- final[i,1]
        j <- j+1
    }
}

Is there any efficient method (single line code) to do the same?

-- 
Thanks & Regards
Sarthi M.

	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [R-sig-DB] R GUI : is there any GUI?
messages.subject_clean: R GUI : is there any GUI?
messages.body_plain:
Hi

Is there any way in R GUI to produce some GUI as we can do in MATLAB?
Can we create some GUI to take some run time user inputs?
Or is there any way to take run-time user input by some mean?

-- 
Thanks & Regards
Sarthi M.

messages.subject: [R-sig-DB] R GUI : any suggestion??
messages.body_plain:
Hi

Please suggest me how to implement "Group by" function of SQL in R GUI?

e.g.
 input data.frame
x                  y
============
a                10
b                20
a                10
a                10
b                15
b                15
b                20

In o/p i need

x            y
=========
a           30
b           70

-- 
Thanks
Sarthi M.

messages.subject: [R-sig-DB] R GUI : any suggestion??
messages.body_plain:
Please stop misusing this list, which is about DBMS interfaces in R.
This is the third irrelevant posting from you this month, in each case 
with a misleading and unspecific title.

R-help is the list for basic R questions, and this has nothing to do with 
any 'R GUI' (nor does this list).

There is a posting guide, and it asks you to do your homework.  Try the 
help on the functions aggregate(), by ()and tapply()

On Wed, 14 Feb 2007, d. sarthi maheshwari wrote:

> Hi

>

> b           70
>
>

-- 
Brian D. Ripley,                  ripley at stats.ox.ac.uk

messages.subject: [R-sig-DB] RSQLite memory leak?
messages.body_plain:
I am using RSQLite 0.4-19 with R 2.4.1 on MacOS X 10.4.8.  Both R and  
RSQLite are compiled from source using Apple's compilers.

The following simple script:
library(DBI)
library(RSQLite)

drv <- dbDriver("SQLite")
con <- dbConnect(drv,trad_db)

dbDisconnect(con)
dbUnloadDriver(drv)

gives the output:
Warning message:
RS-DBI driver warning: (mgr->drvData was not freed (some memory leaked))

This seems to be a meaningful warning as when I run a real script  
with the same beginning and end a number of time R seems to get into  
trouble.

Bill Northcott
messages.body_snippet:

messages.subject: [R-sig-DB] RSQLite memory leak?
messages.body_plain:
Hi Bill,

Bill Northcott <w.northcott at unsw.edu.au> writes:

> I am using RSQLite 0.4-19 with R 2.4.1 on MacOS X 10.4.8.  Both R and  
> RSQLite are compiled from source using Apple's compilers.
>
> The following simple script:
> library(DBI)
> library(RSQLite)
>
> drv <- dbDriver("SQLite")
> con <- dbConnect(drv,trad_db)
>
> dbDisconnect(con)
> dbUnloadDriver(drv)
>
> gives the output:
> Warning message:
> RS-DBI driver warning: (mgr->drvData was not freed (some memory leaked))
>
> This seems to be a meaningful warning as when I run a real script  
> with the same beginning and end a number of time R seems to get into  
> trouble.

Thanks for the report.  I can reproduce the warning and will see if I
can find out what's going on.  

In the meantime, a workaround might be to simply not unload the
driver.  I'm not sure why you would want to unload and reload it in
the first place -- you can reuse the drv object for different
connections.

+ seth

>
>

Seth Falcon <sfalcon at fhcrc.org> writes:
> Hi Bill,
>
> Bill Northcott <w.northcott at unsw.edu.au> writes:
>
>> I am using RSQLite 0.4-19 with R 2.4.1 on MacOS X 10.4.8.  Both R and  
>> RSQLite are compiled from source using Apple's compilers.
>>
>> The following simple script:
>> library(DBI)
>> library(RSQLite)
>>
>> drv <- dbDriver("SQLite")
>> con <- dbConnect(drv,trad_db)
>>
>> dbDisconnect(con)
>> dbUnloadDriver(drv)
>>
>> gives the output:
>> Warning message:
>> RS-DBI driver warning: (mgr->drvData was not freed (some memory leaked))
>>
>> This seems to be a meaningful warning as when I run a real script  
>> with the same beginning and end a number of time R seems to get into  
>> trouble.
>
> Thanks for the report.  I can reproduce the warning and will see if I
> can find out what's going on.  

I believe that I've found and fixed the memory leak with
dbUnloadDriver and have uploaded a new version (0.4-20) to CRAN this
morning.

In the meantime, you can get the updated source package here:

http://bioconductor.org/packages/misc/

+ seth

messages.subject: [R-sig-DB] New version of RSQLite sent to CRAN
messages.subject_clean: New version of RSQLite sent to CRAN
messages.body_plain:
Hi all,

Just a heads up that I've uploaded a new version of RSQLite 0.4-20 to
CRAN this morning (probably won't appear for ~48hrs).  Changes:

* Fix memory leak bug in dbUnloadDriver

* Fix build on Solaris

Updated source package is available here:

    http://bioconductor.org/packages/misc/


+ seth
